questions.question: String quartets, piano trios and instrumental sonatas are common examples, and the independent parts interact closely. What is this?

answers.text: Chamber music
Impressionist
Jazz funk
Lied

questions.hint: The broad category for intimate works played by small instrumental groups.
questions.explanation: String quartets, piano trios and instrumental sonatas are common examples, and the independent parts interact closely.

questions.question: Which is NOT a feature of Chamber music?

answers.text: It is a major or minor triad with a sixth above the root added.
It is written for a small ensemble, usually with one player on each part.
It is usually performed without a conductor.
Its independent parts interact closely, as in a string quartet or piano trio.

questions.hint: C–E–G gains an A.
questions.explanation: The statement “It is a major or minor triad with a sixth above the root added” describes Added 6th, not Chamber music. String quartets, piano trios and instrumental sonatas are common examples, and the independent parts interact closely.

questions.question: Common features include whole-tone or modal scales, unresolved or extended harmony, parallel chords and delicate orchestration. What is this?

answers.text: Sonata
Impressionist
Soul music
String quartet

questions.hint: Blurred pulse, floating harmony and delicate colours.
questions.explanation: Common features include whole-tone or modal scales, unresolved or extended harmony, parallel chords and delicate orchestration.

questions.question: Which is NOT a feature of Impressionist?

answers.text: It is a late-19th- and early-20th-century style focused on atmosphere and tone colour.
It is a four-note chord built from stacked minor thirds.
It may use whole-tone or modal scales, unresolved harmony and parallel chords.
Delicate orchestration, pedal and cross-rhythms may blur pulse and sonority.

questions.hint: Four notes stacked in equal minor thirds.
questions.explanation: The statement “It is a four-note chord built from stacked minor thirds” describes Diminished 7th, not Impressionist. Common features include whole-tone or modal scales, unresolved or extended harmony, parallel chords and delicate orchestration.

Plainchant

It is a three-note chord made from two stacked minor thirds.
Electric piano, guitar, saxophone or synthesiser may play riffs and improvise.

questions.hint: Two minor thirds form a tense three-note chord.
questions.explanation: The statement “It is a three-note chord made from two stacked minor thirds” describes Diminished triad, not Jazz funk. A fusion of jazz harmony and improvisation with funk rhythm.

It is chord V with a minor seventh added above its root.

questions.hint: Four notes built on scale degree five pull strongly towards chord I.
questions.explanation: The statement “It is chord V with a minor seventh added above its root” describes Dominant 7th, not Lied. A German Romantic art song for solo voice and piano.

It sets the fixed sections of the Roman Catholic service to music.

questions.hint: Raise scale degree seven both going up and down.

questions.question: Music constructed from recorded real-world sounds rather than only conventional instruments. What is this?

Musique concrète
Sonata
Soul music

It moves from chord V to chord VI instead of chord I.
Recordings may be cut, reversed, repeated, layered, filtered or transformed.
Playback speed may change, and sound fragments may be spliced or looped.

questions.hint: V heads for I—but lands unexpectedly on VI.
questions.explanation: The statement “It moves from chord V to chord VI instead of chord I” describes Interrupted cadence, not Musique concrète. Music constructed from recorded real-world sounds rather than only conventional instruments.

questions.hint: The musical gap between two pitches.

questions.question: Which is NOT a feature of Plainchant?

Its flexible rhythm follows the text and has no regular bar-based metre.
It raises the sixth and seventh degrees when ascending.

questions.hint: Raise six and seven up; lower them coming down.
questions.explanation: The statement “It raises the sixth and seventh degrees when ascending” describes Melodic minor scale, not Plainchant. Early monophonic sacred vocal music, usually sung in Latin and without instrumental accompaniment.

questions.explanation: The statement “It is a short ornament that moves to a neighbouring note and returns” describes Mordent, not Sonata. A multi-movement work for a solo instrument, or for one instrument with piano.
